subject: Physics
chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A collision takes place between two particles each moving with speed $\mathrm{v}$, the collision is oblique. After collision two blocks move together with velocity $\mathrm{v}^{\prime}$ as shown in figure. Then which of the following option is/ are true :
options: $\tan \phi=\frac{2 \sin \theta}{1+2 \cos \theta}$
$\tan \phi=\frac{2 \cos \theta}{1+2 \sin \theta}$
Final kinetic energy of system $\frac{m v^2}{6}(5+4 \cos \theta)$
loss of kinetic energy $=\frac{2}{3} m v^2(1-\cos \theta)$
questions.solution: $\begin{aligned} & 3 m \vec{v}^{\prime}=(m v+2 m v \cos \theta) \hat{i}+2 m v \sin \theta \hat{j} \\ & \vec{v}^{\prime}=\frac{v}{3}(1+2 \cos \theta) \hat{i}+\frac{2}{3} \sin \theta \hat{j} \\ & \tan \phi=\frac{2 \sin \theta}{1+2 \cos \theta} \\ & \Delta K=k_i-k_f \\ & \frac{3}{2} m v^2-\frac{1}{2}(3 m) v^{\prime 2}=\frac{3}{2} m v^2-\frac{m v^2}{6}(5+4 \cos \theta)\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A canon of mass $M$ is mounted on an east west frictionless railway track. The barrel of cannon faces the north east direction at an angle of $45^{\circ}$ with the vertical. The canon fires a shot of mass $M$ with a speed V relative to the barrel.
options: $\text { The recoil speed of the cannon will be V/4 }$
$\text { The speed of the shot as seen from the ground will be } \frac{\sqrt{13}}{4} \mathrm{~V} \text {. }$
$\text { The momentum of the shot }+ \text { canon system will remain conserved along the east west line. }$
$\text { The momentum of the shot plus canon system will remain conserved along the North South line. }$
questions.solution:
Conserving momentum along the east west line
$\begin{aligned}
& M\left(\operatorname{vos}^2 45-\mathrm{v}_0\right)=M v_0 \\
\Rightarrow & 2 M V_0=M V \cos ^2 45^{\circ} \\
\Rightarrow & v_0=V / 4
\end{aligned}$

Vertical speed $=\frac{v}{\sqrt{2}}$
North speed $=\mathrm{v} \cos ^2 45^{\circ}=\frac{\mathrm{v}}{2}$
East speed $=\frac{V}{2}-\frac{V}{4}=\frac{V}{4}$
$\Rightarrow$ Speed from ground $=v \sqrt{\frac{1}{2}+\frac{1}{4}+\frac{1}{16}}=v \sqrt{\frac{13}{16}}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two blocks A and B, each of mass $m$, are connected by an ideal spring of stiffness $k$ and placed on a smooth horizontal surface. A ball of mass $m$ moving with a velocity $v_0$ strikes the block $A$ and gets embedded to it. Then,
options: velocity of block $A$ just after collision is $v_0 / 2$
velocity of block B just after collision is zero
the maximum compression produced in the spring is $v_0 \sqrt{\frac{m}{6 k}}$
the kinetic energy lost during collision is $\frac{1}{4} m v_0^2$
questions.solution: During the collision imples is not transferred to $\mathrm{B}: m v_0=2 m v^{\prime} \Rightarrow v^{\prime}=\frac{v_0}{2}$ Just after collision $\Rightarrow v_A=\frac{v_0}{2}$ and $v_B=0$ and at the time of maximum compression $\begin{aligned} & v_B=m v_0=3 m v^{\prime \prime} \Rightarrow v^{\prime \prime}=\frac{v_0}{3} \\ & \qquad \frac{1}{2} k x_0^2=\frac{1}{2} 2 m\left(\frac{v_0}{2}\right)^2-\frac{1}{2} 3 m\left(\frac{v_0}{3}\right)^2 \Rightarrow x_0=v_0 \sqrt{\frac{m}{6 k}} \\ & \text { Loss in } \mathrm{KE}=\frac{1}{2} m v_0^2-\frac{1}{2}(2 m)\left(\frac{v_0}{2}\right)^2=\frac{m v_0^2}{4} \end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two balls of masses $M$ and $m$ are attached to two threads of length $I$, such that the two threads are suspended at the same point. The balls are both deflected to the horizontal position of the threads, released from rest at the same time, and they collide head on. (The collision is perfectly elastic.). If the ball of mass m reaches the maximum height which is allowed $M / m$ can be :
questions.solution: $e=1=\frac{v_1+v_2}{2 \sqrt{2 g \ell}}...(i)$ Momentum conservative $M \sqrt{2 g \ell}-m \sqrt{2 g \ell}=m v_1-M v_2...(ii)$ Solving (i) and (ii) $v_1=\left(\frac{3 M-m}{M+m}\right) \sqrt{2 g L} \geq \sqrt{5 g l}$ $\Rightarrow \frac{M}{m} \geq \frac{\sqrt{5}+\sqrt{2}}{3 \sqrt{2}-\sqrt{5}}=1.82=1.82$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A penguin of mass $m$ stands at the right edge of a sled of mass 3 m and legnth $\ell$. The sled lies on frictionless ice. The penguin starts moving towards left, reaches the left end and jumps with a velocity u at an angle $\theta$ with horizontal relative to ground. (Neglect the height of the sled)
options: Till the penguin reaches the left end, the sled is displaced by $\frac{l}{4}$.
Till the penguin reaches the left end, the sled is displaced by $\frac{l}{3}$.
After jumping, it will fall on the ground at a distance $\frac{4}{3} \frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{g}$ from the left end of the sled.
After jumping, it will fall on the ground at a distance $\frac{3}{4} \frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{g}$ from the left end of the sled.
questions.solution:
$\begin{gathered}\text { (A) } \quad \mathrm{S}_{\mathrm{CM}}=\frac{\mathrm{m}_1 \mathrm{~S}_1+\mathrm{m}_2 \mathrm{~S}_2}{\mathrm{~m}_1+\mathrm{m}_2} \\ 0=\frac{(3 \mathrm{~m})(-\mathrm{x})+(\mathrm{m})(\ell-\mathrm{x})}{3 \mathrm{~m}+\mathrm{m}} \\ \mathrm{x}=\frac{l}{4}\end{gathered}$ $\text { (C) }$
$\begin{aligned} & \mathrm{T}=\frac{2 \mathrm{u} \sin \theta}{g} \\ & \text { Displacement of sled in this time }=\left(\frac{u \cos \theta}{3}\right)\left(\frac{2 u \sin \theta}{g}\right)=\frac{1}{3}\left(\frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{g}\right) \end{aligned}$ Total distance between penguin and left end of sled $=\frac{4}{3}\left(\frac{u^2 \sin 2 \theta}{g}\right)$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two spheres of same radii but mass 2 kg and 4 kg are moving toward each other. They collide with restitution coefficient $\mathrm{e}=1 / 3$. During collision the spheres deform and again reform to their original shapes. Then
options: Magnitude of impulse each sphere experienced during deformation is $8 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{Ns}$.
Magnitude of impulse during reformation is $4 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{Ns}$
Loss in kinetic energy of the system during deformation is 18 J .
Loss in kinetic energy of the system during entire collision process is 16 J .
questions.solution: $2 v_1=4 v_2 \Rightarrow v_1=2 v_2, v_1$ and $v_2$ are velocities of A and B along the line of impact after collision $\frac{1}{3}\left(4 \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}+2 \times \frac{\sqrt{3}}{2}\right)=v_1+v_2 \Rightarrow v_2=\frac{1}{\sqrt{3}} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ and $v_1=\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$, Magnitude of impulse during deformation $=4 \sqrt{3} N \cdot S$, Magnitude of impulse during reformation $=\frac{4}{\sqrt{3}} N . S$, Loss in $\mathrm{K} . \mathrm{E}=\frac{1}{2} \times \frac{4}{3} \times(3 \sqrt{3})^2 \times\left(1-e^2\right), 3 \sqrt{3} \mathrm{~m} / \mathrm{s}$ is $v_{\text {red }}$ along line of impact $=16 \mathrm{~T}$.
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: A small ball of mass $m$ is placed in a circular tube of mass $M$ and radius $R$ in gravity free space. Friction is absent between tube and ball. Ball is given a velocity $\mathrm{v}_0$ as shown. Then,
options: Path of ball from centre of mass of system will be circular.
Path of ball from centre of mass of system will be elliptical
Radius of curvature of ball at the time of projection of ball is $\frac{M R}{m+M}$
Normal force between tube and ball if $M=2 m$, at the time of projection of ball is $\frac{2 m V_0^2}{3 R}$
questions.solution:
From centre of mass of system ball and centre of tube moves in circular path.
Radius of curvature of ball at the time of projection of ball is $\frac{M R}{m+M}$ Normal force between tube and ball if $M=2 m$, at the time of projection of ball is
$\begin{aligned} & V_{c m}=\frac{m V_0}{m+M} \\ & N=m\left(\frac{\left(V_0-V_{c m}\right)^2}{r_1}\right)\end{aligned}$
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced

questions.subject: Physics
questions.chapter: Center of Mass Momentum and Collision
questions.examName: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
questions.q: Two blocks of masses $m$ and $4 m$ lie on a smooth horizontal surface connected with a spring in its natural length. Mass $m$ is given velocity $v_0$ through an impulse as shown in figure. Which of the following is true about subsequent motion?
options: Kinetic energy is maximum in ground frame and centre of mass (CM) frame simultaneously.
Value of maximum and minimum kinetic energy is same in CM and ground frame.
Minimum kinetic energy is zero in CM frame but non-zero in ground frame.
Maximum and minimum kinetic energy of $m$ in ground frame is, respectively, $\frac{1}{2} m v_0^2$ and zero
questions.solution:
In both CM and ground frame, $\mathrm{K}_{\max }$ is there, when x is zero in spring, which occurs simultaneously.
$v_{C M}=\frac{m\left(v_0\right)+0}{5 m}=\frac{v_0}{5}$
$K_{\max C M}=\frac{1}{2} m\left(\frac{4 v_0}{5}\right)^2+\frac{1}{2}(4 m)\left(\frac{v_0}{5}\right)^2=\frac{2}{5} m v_0^2$
$K_{\max ground}=\frac{1}{2} m v_{\circ}^2$
$K_{\min \mathrm{CM}}=0$
$K_{\min ground}=\frac{1}{2}(m+4 m) v_{C M}^2=\frac{m v_0^2}{10}$
$K_{\max m}=\frac{1}{2} m v_0^2 \quad$ (ground frame)
$K_{\min }=0$ (ground frame when energy is shared by spring and 4 m and m will reverse direction of motion).
questions.source: Most Important Selected Qs for JEE Advanced
